title: elymus alaskanus subsp. subalpinus
intro: Elymus alaskanus subsp. subalpinus, also known as Alaskan wildrye, is a subspecies of grass native to the mountainous regions of North America. The medicinal properties of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Elymus alaskanus subsp. subalpinus include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. The plant is traditionally used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
